title: Canonical queue snapshot versus additive operational state

memory.observed: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json was last updated on 2026-07-11 and marks M-001 completed, while later continuity commits and records identify M-RECONCILE-002 as ongoing work.
memory.inferred: The repository contains a canonical queue snapshot plus newer additive operational projections. Reading either layer alone can produce an incorrect current-state conclusion.
memory.proposed: Queue selection must read ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as the canonical base, then reconcile later provenance-bound queue updates and continuity records by commit time, declared authority, and exact-byte receipt.
memory.superseded: The latest-looking queue artifact is sufficient to determine current ownership and priority regardless of authority or provenance.
memory.unresolved: No single authoritative materialized queue currently proves that all additive updates through the inspected commit horizon have been enumerated and promoted.

contradiction_resolution.canonical_claim: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json contains only completed M-001 for continuity_mining.
contradiction_resolution.later_claim: Recent continuity artifacts identify M-RECONCILE-002 as active reconciliation work.
contradiction_resolution.resolution: The claims apply to different state layers: the first describes the canonical base snapshot; the second describes newer additive operational state.
contradiction_resolution.selection_rule: Prefer a schema-valid, base-bound, authoritative materialization. Otherwise retain the canonical base and label later ownership as an effective noncanonical projection with unresolved completeness.

falsification_route: Produce an authoritative base-bound materialized queue covering every additive update through the stated commit horizon. Revise the effective-state inference if that queue identifies a different current continuity item.
